edges.description: Profound fetal neuromuscular transmission failure produces the fetal akinesia/hypokinesia sequence.
Prolonged reduced fetal movement leads to prenatal joint contractures.
Severe fetal akinesia leads to multiple congenital contractures across body regions.
The lethal fetal akinesia sequence includes multiple pterygia across affected joint regions.
Severe fetal akinesia/lethal multiple pterygium presentations can include cystic hygroma.
intermediate_mechanisms: Fetal edema in lethal multiple pterygium syndrome

edges.description: Severe fetal akinesia/lethal multiple pterygium presentations can include fetal hydrops.
intermediate_mechanisms: Fetal edema in lethal multiple pterygium syndrome

edges.description: Fetal akinesia deformation sequence can include micrognathia.
intermediate_mechanisms: Abnormal intrauterine environment restricting fetal movement

edges.description: Fetal akinesia deformation sequence can include pulmonary hypoplasia, contributing to lethality.
intermediate_mechanisms: Abnormal intrauterine environment restricting fetal movement

nodes.description: Multiple congenital contractures across body regions are characteristic of the severe fetal akinesia presentation.

evidence.reference_title: Case Report: Novel compound heterozygous variants in CHRNA1 gene leading to lethal multiple pterygium syndrome: A case report
evidence.snippet: Case presentation: We herein report a Chinese case with a history of three adverse pregnancies demonstrating the same ultrasonic phenotypes, including increased nuchal translucency, edema, fetal neck cystoma, reduced movement, joint contractures, and other congenital features.

evidence.reference_title: Acetylcholine receptor pathway mutations explain various fetal akinesia deformation sequence disorders
evidence.snippet: We provide the first report to our knowledge of homozygous nonsense mutations in CHRNA1 and CHRND and show that they were lethal, whereas novel recessive missense mutations in RAPSN caused a severe but not necessarily lethal phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Case Report: Novel compound heterozygous variants in CHRNA1 gene leading to lethal multiple pterygium syndrome: A case report
evidence.snippet: Conclusion: For the first time, we identified an association between the CHRNA1 gene and the recurrent lethal multiple pterygium syndrome (LMPS) in a Chinese family.

nodes.description: Nuchal cystic hygroma or neck cystoma may be detected on prenatal imaging in severely affected fetuses.

evidence.reference_title: Case Report: Novel compound heterozygous variants in CHRNA1 gene leading to lethal multiple pterygium syndrome: A case report
evidence.snippet: Case presentation: We herein report a Chinese case with a history of three adverse pregnancies demonstrating the same ultrasonic phenotypes, including increased nuchal translucency, edema, fetal neck cystoma, reduced movement, joint contractures, and other congenital features.

nodes.description: Biallelic null or functionally severe CHRNA1 variants disrupt the alpha-1 subunit of the fetal muscle nicotinic acetylcholine receptor, preventing normal postsynaptic receptor function at the neuromuscular junction. This produces profound impairment of fetal neuromuscular transmission and places CHRNA1-related disease on a spectrum with later-onset congenital myasthenic syndrome caused by milder alleles.

evidence.reference_title: Acetylcholine receptor pathway mutations explain various fetal akinesia deformation sequence disorders
evidence.snippet: We provide the first report to our knowledge of homozygous nonsense mutations in CHRNA1 and CHRND and show that they were lethal, whereas novel recessive missense mutations in RAPSN caused a severe but not necessarily lethal phenotype.

nodes.description: Markedly reduced fetal movement is the core prenatal manifestation of the disorder and drives the secondary contracture and malformation sequence.

evidence.reference_title: Case Report: Novel compound heterozygous variants in CHRNA1 gene leading to lethal multiple pterygium syndrome: A case report
evidence.snippet: Case presentation: We herein report a Chinese case with a history of three adverse pregnancies demonstrating the same ultrasonic phenotypes, including increased nuchal translucency, edema, fetal neck cystoma, reduced movement, joint contractures, and other congenital features.

nodes.description: Profound reduction of fetal neuromuscular transmission leads to markedly decreased fetal movement. Secondary consequences include multiple joint contractures, pterygia, edema or hydrops, craniofacial anomalies such as micrognathia, and pulmonary hypoplasia, yielding a prenatal-lethal multiple-pterygium / fetal akinesia deformation sequence presentation.

evidence.reference_title: Acetylcholine receptor pathway mutations explain various fetal akinesia deformation sequence disorders
evidence.snippet: We conclude that complete or severe functional disruption of fetal AChR causes lethal multiple pterygium syndrome whereas milder alterations result in fetal hypokinesia with inborn contractures or a myasthenic syndrome later in life.

nodes.description: Generalized fetal edema or hydrops can occur in the most severe prenatal presentations.

evidence.reference_title: Case Report: Novel compound heterozygous variants in CHRNA1 gene leading to lethal multiple pterygium syndrome: A case report
evidence.snippet: Case presentation: We herein report a Chinese case with a history of three adverse pregnancies demonstrating the same ultrasonic phenotypes, including increased nuchal translucency, edema, fetal neck cystoma, reduced movement, joint contractures, and other congenital features.

nodes.description: Fixed prenatal contractures involving multiple joints reflect prolonged lack of fetal movement and contribute to the arthrogryposis phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Case Report: Novel compound heterozygous variants in CHRNA1 gene leading to lethal multiple pterygium syndrome: A case report
evidence.snippet: Case presentation: We herein report a Chinese case with a history of three adverse pregnancies demonstrating the same ultrasonic phenotypes, including increased nuchal translucency, edema, fetal neck cystoma, reduced movement, joint contractures, and other congenital features.

nodes.description: Small mandible is a recurrent craniofacial feature in the prenatal-lethal CHRNA1 presentation.

evidence.reference_title: Fetal akinesia deformation sequence in previable fetuses
evidence.snippet: In eight fetuses who had joint contractures, micrognathia, and pulmonary hypoplasia, the cause of fetal akinesia could be attributed to an abnormal intrauterine environment restricting fetal movement.

nodes.description: Multiple webbing anomalies across joints are typical of the lethal multiple pterygium syndrome presentation.

evidence.reference_title: Case Report: Novel compound heterozygous variants in CHRNA1 gene leading to lethal multiple pterygium syndrome: A case report
evidence.snippet: Background: Lethal multiple pterygium syndrome (LMPS) is a rare autosomal recessive inherited disorder typically characterized by intrauterine growth retardation, multiple pterygia, and flexion contractures.

nodes.description: Pulmonary underdevelopment is part of the fetal akinesia deformation sequence and contributes to lethality.

evidence.reference_title: Fetal akinesia deformation sequence in previable fetuses
evidence.snippet: In eight fetuses who had joint contractures, micrognathia, and pulmonary hypoplasia, the cause of fetal akinesia could be attributed to an abnormal intrauterine environment restricting fetal movement.
